headline: Defense bill dominance continues as Senate locks in three judicial confirmations!

senate.lastSessionSummary: The Senate confirmed Kara Marie Westercamp to the Court of International Trade by a 50-48 vote and passed the Affordable Prescriptions For Patients Act addressing patent infringement of biological products. A unanimous-consent agreement was reached scheduling judicial confirmation votes for Wednesday, including Benjamin M. Flowers for the Sixth Circuit.

onTheFloor.title: Affordable Prescriptions For Patients Act—amends patent law to address infringement claims involving biological products
Authorizes use of Capitol Rotunda for ceremony honoring late Senator Lindsey Graham
Designates June 15 as World Elder Abuse Awareness Day and June as Elder Abuse Awareness Month
Constitutes majority party membership on committees for 119th Congress
Benjamin M. Flowers of Ohio to be United States Circuit Judge for the Sixth Circuit
Michael C. Martin of Michigan to be United States District Judge for Eastern District of Michigan
Antonio M. Pozos of Pennsylvania to be United States District Judge for Eastern District of Pennsylvania
onTheFloor.status: cloture vote scheduled after Martin confirmation

onTheFloor.title: Daniel Mack Traynor of North Dakota to be United States Circuit Judge for the Eighth Circuit

house.lastSessionSummary: The House passed H.R. 1118, the Value Over Cost Act, by 421-1 establishing best value procurement through the multiple award schedule program. Consideration of H.R. 8800, the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027, continues with dozens of amendments adopted addressing military readiness, supply chain security, and personnel support.

onTheFloor.title: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027—authorizes appropriations for military activities, construction, and defense operations
Value Over Cost Act—provides best value procurement through multiple award schedule program
Rogers en bloc amendment package including 90+ provisions on military modernization, supply chain security, servicemember benefits, and semiconductor acquisitions
Begich amendments requiring 72-hour cyber incident reporting and Chinese sourcing restrictions on defense contractors
Buchanan and other amendments providing servicemember benefits including glucose monitoring, nutrition programs, and mental health services

committees.committee: Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation
committees.topic: S. 1759 civil supersonic flight, S. 3258 FAA medication list, S. 4429 foreign adversary connected vehicles, and nominations including NTSB and TSA administrator—10:30 AM Wednesday

committees.committee: Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions
committees.topic: S. 2355 hospital price transparency, S. 4189 insulin pricing, S. 5026 food and beverage warning labels, and nursing workforce development—10:00 AM Wednesday
Nominations for multiple ambassadors and assistant secretaries including Kari Lake for Jamaica and Douglas Mastriano for Slovak Republic—9:00 AM Wednesday
Subcommittee on Communications and Technology hearing on protecting communications networks and improving connectivity—1:30 PM Wednesday
Full committee continues markup on H.R. 9086, 9062, 1939, and 9087 regarding Foreign Service Act, international trade, Sudan sanctions, and foreign affairs matters—5:00 PM Wednesday
Full committee hearing on funding lapses and shutdown reform—10:00 AM Wednesday
